events.title: Born in Ulm, Germany
events.description: A child is born who will one day reshape our understanding of the universe.

events.title: Swiss Polytechnic
events.description: He enrols at the Zürich Polytechnic, renouncing German citizenship and pursuing physics and mathematics.

events.title: Quiet Beginnings
events.description: Working in Bern’s Patent Office, Einstein develops ideas that challenge classical physics.

events.title: The Miracle Year
events.description: In a single year, he publishes four papers introducing special relativity and E = mc².

events.title: General Relativity
events.description: Einstein presents the General Theory of Relativity, redefining gravity as curved spacetime.

events.title: Light Bends, Fame Rises
events.description: A solar eclipse confirms his theory, and his name echoes across the globe.

events.title: Nobel Prize
events.description: Honoured for the photoelectric effect, his brilliance receives global acclaim.

events.title: New Life in America
events.description: Fleeing Nazi Germany, Einstein settles in Princeton and continues his research in exile.

events.title: Letter to Roosevelt
events.description: He warns the U.S. government about atomic research, influencing early nuclear development.

events.title: A New Citizenship
events.description: Einstein becomes an American citizen, beginning a new chapter far from home.

events.title: Final Chapter
events.description: He dies in Princeton, leaving behind a legacy that reshaped physics and human thought.
